headline: Minutes of meditation, measurably less pain
subtext: Four short sessions changed how the brain processed pain.
caption:
Could a few minutes of training change how much pain you feel? A neuroscience study suggests it can.

After just four days of brief mindfulness training, volunteers facing a painful stimulus reported pain intensity down by 40% and unpleasantness down by 57%, compared with rest. Brain imaging showed the change was real, with altered activity in regions that build the experience of pain.

This is not about ignoring pain. It is about changing the brain's relationship to it, the same principle that underlies recovery from chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID.

Work with a practitioner who teaches these skills at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: The study: Zeidan F et al. (2011), Brain mechanisms supporting the modulation of pain by mindfulness meditation, Journal of Neuroscience, 31(14), 5540 to 5548. Brief mindfulness training reduced pain intensity by 40% and unpleasantness by 57%, with corresponding changes in brain activity. https://www.jneurosci.org/content/31/14/5540
